What's going on guys? Okay, so I just want to give you the kind of rundown of how the green bribe EFT play works. It basically combines two of my favorite plays to a single play. So the EFT phone play was one of my all-time favorite plays and obviously there's a secret challenge play. The upside of the phone conversion play is that everyone's going to rate an EFT and you have a more, you just have a higher percentage close on leads with that selling mechanism of phone sales. The advantages of the green bribe EFT play are that you have all of the lead-gen capacity of the six-week free play but you have the conversion of the EFT on the back end and that's why this excites me so much. So this is kind of the pros and cons, right? Pros for the six-week free as you get cashed in front, you get lots of leads and ALN does cut the work down. The cons is that the front end sale does require some skill, you have to do an abandoned hope and then you still have some collection that you said sometimes come up, right? Six-week EFT phone play, you have the lifetime value of the EFT which is really high. You have a higher percentage conversion leads because it's over the phone. The cons are, again, it requires skill. There's less leads and there's lots of work working the leads, right? Less leads because the offer of not free is not as good as free and then lots of work looking leads because we can't use ALN for the phone play. Now, here's the beauty of the green free bribe play is that you're still marketing a six-week free play but you're getting all the lifetime value of the EFT. You're getting tons of leads because it's free. A higher percentage conversion leads because the offer when they walk in the door is better. You get an assumed close because you're going to give them an option and whenever you give people two options, they pick between the options. They're not taking into account the third option which is that they don't buy, right? We can give them either or close. It's scalable selling because it's an easier offer and an easier pitch. More people can do which means it's less reliant on some superstar salesperson. There are some gems right now that are running this that don't even pay commissions to their front desk person for signing people up with this because it's that easy. This one also uses ALN. It has the leverage of not needing to have to work the lead. The cons is that you get less cash upfront, sort of, but I can explain in more math why it's actually almost the same. The truth about six-week free versus LBOs is that LBOs actually do work great if you're great. What I mean by that is that the reason that they don't work is because the lifetime value of each person is low. Most gems don't actually have very high lifetime value per customer which is why the front-loaded cash works well for them. What that means from a real world standpoint would be we're asking someone to pay for their food first at a restaurant and then we give them food and then because they don't keep coming back to the restaurant, we just charge as much as we can before they have the food so that we can make as much on a promise rather than on the actual product. That's why most gems don't do well. We can help you fix that with the cash flow upfront from the challenges, but long-term you do have to fix the recurring. The yellows are inputs which we do touch and then these arms are like kind of the hard numbers that we're looking at. And then you're making $450 if you take out the 10% of refunds, and then you're converting 50% of those people into EFT at $200 a month. And if your monthly turn is 10%, then it means total lifetime value over a year would be $16,000. So this one play for 100 leads would make you $16,800 by month 12. Let's say both gyms have a 10% turn, your 12 month lifetime value from both of these plays, one is 16, one is 36, they're making twice as much money. Okay. Now that's assuming you have the same turn. The thing is, is that if you have everyone going straight into EFT, the likelihood that you'll be able to focus on your actual product, your service that we're selling is a lot higher because then you don't have challengers and members, you just have members, because every new comes in is just trying to spread into the EFT as a member. And so all you're doing is you're showing your members with lots of love, right? Right. And so my guess is that over time, this actually would go down significantly, even if we did the same percentages, you're still making more money with the green play.
